svg.title: Figure 5. Synthetic report-side scale conversion after finite opportunity-scale detection
svg.desc: Formula-style diagram showing a report-side conversion only after a finite opportunity-scale public-signal diagnostic exists. The input is T_detect equals 1 comparable abstract opportunity. The adjacent conversion note says synthetic, analyst-supplied q_spot=0.01 is not estimated by the toolkit, and ceil(1/0.01)=100 is displayed as physical-hand scale units.
svg.text: Formula diagram only; no countdown, timeline, axis tick, or standalone badge for the converted value.
q_spot is not estimated by the toolkit
converted display unit: physical-hand scale units
synthetic, analyst-supplied q_spot=0.01; q_spot is not estimated by the toolkit
This is a synthetic scale-conversion example, not a real-game occurrence estimate.
Caption. Synthetic physical-hand scale conversion is applied only after a finite opportunity-scale public-signal diagnostic exists.
In this calibration row, T_detect=1 comparable abstract opportunity. With synthetic, analyst-supplied q_spot=0.01, not estimated by the toolkit,
the report-side conversion displays ceil(1/0.01)=100 physical-hand scale units.
Footnote. The numeric conversion is a synthetic scale-conversion example: q_spot=0.01 is analyst-supplied and not estimated by the toolkit,
and ceil(1/0.01)=100 is displayed only as physical-hand scale units.
